spec_reconstruction.domain: U.S. federal banking law/governance — board-level obligations of Category I GSIB bank holding companies and their insured depository institution (IDI) subsidiaries
spec_reconstruction.unit_of_analysis: Individual board-level obligation (with regulatory citation, nature of duty, actor mapping, director-status distinction, and duplication/reform analysis)
structural_axes: Entity tier: BHC vs. IDI subsidiary (national bank, state member, state nonmember, CIDI)
Actor class: full board vs. risk committee vs. audit committee vs. compensation committee vs. nominating/governance committee
Director status: inside vs. outside; independent vs. non-independent; risk expert; exchange-listing-independent
Source tier: binding regulation / enforceable safety-and-soundness guideline / supervisory guidance / examination procedure
Regulatory domain (capital, liquidity, resolution, recovery, op resilience, model risk, third-party, incentive comp, cyber, BSA/AML, consumer compliance, safety & soundness)
source_universe: Federal Reserve (Reg YY, Reg Y, Reg Q, SR letters, LFI rating)
OCC (Heightened Standards Appendix D, Appendix A safety & soundness, Appendix B info security, Appendix E recovery [rescinded 2026], Part 21 security/BSA, OCC bulletins)
FDIC (Part 363 FDICIA audit, Part 360.10 CIDI resolution, Part 364 info security, Part 326 BSA)
Interagency standards (third-party 2023, information security GLBA, incentive comp 2010, model risk SR 26-2)
Statutes (Dodd-Frank §165 EPS implicit, FDIA §39 explicit, BSA implicit)
coverage_areas: Consumer compliance (CFPB CMR, UDAAP, fair lending)
spec_reconstruction.audience_use: Bank directors and their legal counsel; policymakers engaged in reform aimed at reducing duplicative/overlapping/unduly burdensome board-level requirements; explicitly self-described as a working policy-reform inventory
spec_reconstruction.form_depth_expectations: Granular, citation-specific, comprehensive inventory; ~40–60 pages; trace each obligation to source/nature/actor/director-status; include cross-domain matrix

material_omissions: No dedicated nominating/governance committee section despite being an explicit required actor class — board-composition, succession-planning, and director-nomination duties not separately treated
Source-of-strength doctrine (12 CFR §225.4(a)(1)) — a foundational Fed board obligation — is not cited
Regulation O / Section 22(h) insider-lending board approval (12 CFR Part 215, §337.3) and conflicts-of-interest framework absent
12 U.S.C. §1818 director removal, civil-money-penalty tiers, and industry-wide prohibition (the principal enforcement-exposure scaffolding for board members) not addressed
31 U.S.C. §5318 BSA statutory grounding — including §5318(g) SAR/tipping-off personal liability — not cited; only the implementing regulations are
12 CFR §21.11(h) per-SAR-filing board notification (and (h)(2) non-suspect-director notification for insider-abuse SARs) absent

OCC Bulletin 2025-51 (proposed $700B Heightened Standards threshold) defensibly excluded under the methodology's 'proposed rules excluded' rule, but the methodology's choice arguably suppresses material reform context

SEC oversight requirements (Item 106 cyber, Item 407 audit/nom/comp committee disclosure) explicitly excluded by methodology — defensible but reduces fitness for the stated 'directors and counsel' audience

Inside vs. outside director treatment is solid at the FDICIA audit-committee level but is not run as a systematic axis across other domains (e.g., Reg YY risk committee independence is described, but the inside/outside cut is not consistently applied)

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.33(a) BHC risk committee (independent chair, risk expert, quarterly review)
results.reason: eCFR confirms independent-chair structure (S-K Item 407 / exchange listing standards) and risk-management experience requirement.

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.33(a)(1) risk committee responsibility for liquidity
results.reason: Cross-reference between risk committee subpart and liquidity rule (§252.34) accurately characterized.

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.34(a) board approval of liquidity risk tolerance and policies
results.reason: Reg YY liquidity provisions confirmed.

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.34(b) risk committee CFP approval at least annually
results.reason: Risk committee or designated subcommittee approval of contingency funding plan verified.

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.34(c) at least quarterly liquidity reporting to board or risk committee
results.reason: Reg YY reporting cadence accurate.

results.citation: 12 CFR 252.35 liquidity stress testing and buffer
results.reason: Stress testing and buffer requirement accurately summarized.

results.citation: 12 CFR 225.8 capital plan and stress capital buffer
results.reason: Reg Y capital plan rule and SCB process verified.

results.citation: 12 CFR Part 217 advanced approaches board approval and audit reporting
results.reason: Advanced approaches implementation plan board approval and §217.122(i) annual audit reporting exist.

results.citation: 12 CFR 243.4(f) / 381.4(f) Title I resolution plan board approval and minutes
results.reason: Joint Fed/FDIC rule requires board approval pre-submission and minutes notation.

results.citation: 12 CFR 360.10 FDIC CIDI resolution plan (with GSIB-affiliated biennial cycle under 2024 final rule)
results.reason: 2024 FDIC final rule confirmed: GSIB-affiliated CIDIs biennial starting 2026; others triennial starting 2025.

results.citation: 12 CFR 363.5(a)(1) $5B+ IDI audit committee — outside directors independent of management
results.reason: eCFR confirms verbatim, including January 1, 2026 threshold increase from $1B to $5B.

results.citation: 12 CFR 363.5(a)(2) $1B-<$5B audit committee — outside directors, majority independent of management
results.reason: Outside-director composition with majority-independent-of-management requirement verified.

results.citation: 12 CFR 21.21 / 208.63 / 326.8 BSA program board approval and minutes notation (OCC/Fed/FDIC parallel)
results.reason: All three parallel BSA program regulations exist and require board-approved, minuted programs.

results.citation: 12 CFR Part 30 Appendix D OCC Heightened Standards — risk governance framework, risk appetite, independent directors
results.reason: Appendix D content and at-least-two independent directors expectation verified.

results.citation: 12 CFR Part 30 Appendix B / Part 364 Appendix B / Part 225 Appendix F — Interagency GLBA information security guidelines (board program approval)
results.reason: Parallel codification across OCC/FDIC/Fed accurate; board/committee approval of written info-security program required.

results.citation: 12 CFR Part 21 Subpart A — security officer annual report to board with substance in minutes
results.reason: 12 CFR §21.4 reporting requirement matches Report B's characterization.

results.citation: 12 CFR Part 53 / 12 CFR 225 Subpart N / 12 CFR 304 Subpart C — 36-hour computer-security incident notification
results.reason: Cross-agency computer-security incident notification rule exists; characterization accurate.

results.citation: SR 15-18 Category I capital planning supervisory expectations
results.reason: SR 15-18 confirmed as applying to Category I firms; governance, internal controls, capital policy, scenario design, projection methodology characterization accurate.

results.citation: SR 23-4 / OCC Bulletin 2023-17 / FDIC FIL-29-2023 — 2023 Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships
results.reason: Federal Reserve issued the June 7, 2023 interagency TPRM guidance as SR 23-4; all three agency bulletin numbers verified.

results.citation: OCC Appendix E to Part 30 recovery planning guidelines treated as RESCINDED and excluded from current obligations
results.reason: OCC finalized rescission on March 31, 2026 (Federal Register April 1, 2026); Report B's exclusion is current and correct.

actor_mapping.note: Each obligation is tagged with a specific actor — BHC full board vs. BHC risk committee vs. IDI full board vs. IDI audit committee vs. CIDI board vs. compensation committee — and the cross-domain matrix in Chapter 13 makes the actor cut explicit. Director-status terminology gets its own dedicated chapter (Chapter 12) which is a strength. Gap: nominating/governance committee is not separately treated, and the inside/outside cut is not consistently applied across non-audit domains.

binding_vs_guidance.note: Every obligation table column-tags status as 'Binding rule,' 'Enforceable guideline,' 'Supervisory guidance,' or 'Examination procedure.' The methodology section explains the four-tier source hierarchy. Proposed rules and Appendix E (rescinded) are explicitly excluded with reasons. The 'guidance-driven hardening' note in Chapter 14 frames the de-facto-rule problem honestly.

reform_usefulness.note: Chapter 14 categorizes burden (parent-subsidiary duplication, approval proliferation, committee overlap, board-management boundary, terminology, guidance hardening); Chapter 15 maps each to a reform lever; Appendix E gives a practical board calendar; Appendix I provides an A–H coding key for classifying obligations. This is the strongest reform-actionable framing in the bake-off.

critical_flags.evidence: Nominating/governance committee — an explicit ORIGINAL_REQUEST actor class — is not separately treated; Reg O insider transactions and §1818 director enforcement framework are absent. The methodology defensibly excludes SEC/exchange-listing/SOX/Delaware fiduciary duties, but the scope choice does leave gaps. Partial scope evasion, not material.

D1_coverage.evidence: "The inventory excludes proposed rules, exchange-listing rules, SEC proxy rules, state corporate-law fiduciary duties, private contractual obligations, confidential supervisory findings, and firm-specific enforcement actions."
D1_coverage.rationale: All named coverage areas (capital, liquidity, resolution, recovery, op resilience, model risk, TPRM, incentive comp, cyber, BSA/AML, consumer compliance, safety & soundness) are present and most are deeply covered. Material gaps: no dedicated nominating/governance committee section; §225.4(a)(1) source of strength not cited; Reg O / §337.3 insider transactions absent; §1818 enforcement framework absent; §21.11(h) per-SAR board notification absent. F6 peripheral applies but does not cap (not material scope evasion).

D2_integrity.evidence: "OCC recovery-planning guidelines formerly located at 12 CFR part 30, appendix E are treated as rescinded and are not counted as current in-force obligations."
D2_integrity.rationale: 20/20 sampled citations SUPPORTS, zero unverifiable. Currency exceptional — captures the March 31, 2026 Appendix E rescission, the 2026 effective date of the FDICIA $1B→$5B threshold change, and the GSIB-affiliated CIDI biennial 2026 cycle. SR 23-4 (TPRM) and SR 15-18 (Category I capital planning) verified to exact agency identifiers. Justifies exemplary score.

D3_accuracy.evidence: "Supervisory guidance and examination manuals generally do not create binding law, but they often shape examiner expectations, board reporting packages, and governance burdens in practice."
D3_accuracy.rationale: Substantive characterizations align with primary sources: independent-chair definition under §252.33, four-tier GLBA codification, FDICIA outside-director vs. independent-of-management terminology, GSIB CIDI biennial cycle, Appendix E rescission. No instance of guidance presented as binding law; binding-vs-guidance distinction is explicitly column-tagged on every table. No F3 (stale) or F4 (invented obligation) found.

D4_sludge_analysis.evidence: "Code A: Nondelegable board approval … Code G: Legal-entity duplication … Code H: Terminology conflict."
D4_sludge_analysis.rationale: Six structural burden categories → eight reform levers → A–H coding key → practical board calendar. Reform recommendations are concrete (e.g., 'permit common enterprise resolution board package with entity-specific approval pages,' 'harmonize director-status glossary'), name specific instruments, and preserve accountability rather than recommending across-the-board deregulation. Best-in-class reform-actionable framing.

D5_structure.evidence: "RES-003 — CIDI resolution submission board approval … Primary source 12 CFR 360.10 … Board actor: Covered IDI board"
D5_structure.rationale: Highly granular: 14 chapters, cross-domain matrix, source register with URLs, ~60 obligation cards with structured fields (Legal status / Primary source / Board actor / Duty summary / Evidence artifact / Overlap issue / Reform treatment). Falls short of 4 because: (a) Appendix H repeats the same 'Policy-reform coding' boilerplate paragraph 11 times verbatim, indicating template fill rather than calibrated drafting; (b) no dedicated nominating/governance committee section; (c) some chapter tables run citations without subsection pin-cites (e.g., '12 CFR part 217' alone).

D6_calibration.evidence: "This document is a working policy-reform inventory, not legal advice … It should be validated by counsel against the current Code of Federal Regulations …"
D6_calibration.rationale: Methodology section explicitly defines what is in and out of scope; Appendix C ('Preliminary Open Issues for Counsel Validation') and Appendix F ('Suggested Counsel Review Questions') treat the inventory as a starting point requiring validation; status column on every table prevents guidance-as-rule confusion; no false-exhaustiveness claims (Report deliberately avoids the word 'comprehensive' in favor of 'working draft'). No F5.

strengths: D2 — citation integrity is the strongest in the bake-off so far: 20/20 sampled citations SUPPORTS, including the March 31, 2026 OCC Appendix E rescission, the 2026 FDICIA §363.5 threshold change, and the GSIB-affiliated CIDI biennial 2026 cycle under the 2024 FDIC final rule. The report is current through the report date.

D3 / binding-vs-guidance — every table column-tags 'Binding rule / Enforceable guideline / Supervisory guidance / Examination procedure,' and the executive summary and Chapter 14 explicitly flag the 'guidance-driven hardening' problem rather than collapsing the categories.

D4 / reform usefulness — Chapter 15's eight reform levers, the A–H coding key in Appendix I, and the practical board calendar in Appendix E together give the report a true working-document character; the framing 'preserve accountability, reduce ritual' is concretely operationalized.

D6 / calibration — methodology section explicitly excludes proposed rules, SEC/exchange/SOX/Delaware items, and confidential supervisory findings, with reasons; the 'Important Note on Use' and the counsel-validation appendices keep the epistemic posture honest.

Structural axes — Chapter 12 'Director Status, Independence, and Committee Composition' is the single best treatment of the inside/outside/independent/expert terminology problem across the four reports, and the cross-domain matrix in Chapter 13 makes the actor x domain x approval cut visible at a glance.
weaknesses: D1 / F6 (peripheral) — nominating/governance committee, an explicitly required actor class in the ORIGINAL_REQUEST, has no dedicated section; succession-planning and director-nomination duties are not separately enumerated even though SR 21-3 and OCC Appendix D both create them.

D1 — source-of-strength doctrine (12 CFR §225.4(a)(1)), Regulation O insider-transaction board approval (12 CFR Part 215 / §337.3), and the §1818 director-removal / CMP / industry-prohibition framework are absent — collectively, the principal individual-director enforcement scaffolding is missing.

D1 — BSA section is regulation-only: 31 U.S.C. §5318(g) tipping-off personal liability, §5318(h) program statutory grounding, and §21.11(h) per-SAR board notification (a hard codified board duty) are not addressed.
D5 — Appendix H repeats an identical 'Policy-reform coding:' paragraph verbatim 11 times across its source-by-source notes, which reads as template fill and dilutes an otherwise strong structure.

D6 (minor) — the inventory's defensible exclusion of SEC, exchange-listing, and SOX obligations limits fitness for the named audience of 'bank directors and their legal counsel,' even though the methodology defends the scope.
prioritized_remediation: Add a dedicated Chapter on nominating/governance committee obligations (succession planning under SR 21-3, OCC Appendix D talent/CEO appointment, board-self-assessment cadence, director-nomination process).
Add a §1818 / director-liability chapter: removal, CMP tiers, industry-wide prohibition, six-year post-resignation continuing jurisdiction, and the §1818(b)(8) CAMELS-management linkage.
Add the Regulation O / §22(h) insider-lending board-approval regime (12 CFR Part 215, §337.3) and Source-of-Strength doctrine (§225.4(a)(1)) — both are core BHC board duties.
Strengthen BSA section with 31 U.S.C. §5318(g)/(h) statutory grounding and the 12 CFR §21.11(h) per-SAR board-notification rule including the (h)(2) non-suspect-director carve-out.
Replace the repeated 'Policy-reform coding' boilerplate in Appendix H with source-specific reform analysis to match the granularity of the obligation cards in Appendix D.
narrative.reliance_recommendation: Reliable as a working policy-reform inventory and as a starting brief for counsel review of duplication and burden across federal banking obligations on Category I GSIB boards; the citation integrity and currency are strong enough to ground reform advocacy, but counsel should supplement with the Reg O / §1818 / nominating-committee gaps before relying on this as a complete director-duties map.
